request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will Bitcoin be above $109,999.99 by Dec 31, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET?
Kind: active
Timeline (20 events):
- 2026-05-15 [background] CME | Implementation of the Nasdaq CME Crypto Index Futures Market Maker Program | [2] — CME's market-maker program can boost crypto futures liquidity and upward pressure on BTC, modestly raising chance CF spot exceeds $109,999.99.
- 2026-05-14 [A] CME | Implementation of the Bitcoin Volatility Futures Market Maker Program | [2] — CME's Bitcoin volatility futures market‑maker program may change liquidity and volatility in derivatives markets, indirectly nudging Bitcoin spot price probability of exceeding $109,999.
- 2026-05-13 [A] CME | Expansion of the Trading and ClearingHours for all Cryptocurrency Futures and Options on Futures Contracts. | [1] — Longer CME trading/clearing hours for crypto futures can modestly boost liquidity and price discovery, slightly increasing the chance BTC exceeds $109,999.99.
- 2026-05-13 [background] CME | Implementation of the Cryptocurrency Options 24/7 Weekend Market MakerProgram | [2] — CME’s 24/7 weekend options MM program can modestly increase crypto liquidity and trading continuity, slightly raising odds BTC hits $110k.
- 2026-05-08 [A] Self-Regulatory Organizations; The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC; Notice of Filing of Proposed Rule Change, as Modified by Amendment No. 1 Thereto, to List and Trade Shares of iShares Bitcoin Premium Income ETF under Nasdaq Rule 5711(d) (CommodityBased Trust... — SEC filing to list a BlackRock iShares Bitcoin ETF (with Coinbase/BNY Mellon involved) could boost spot demand and liquidity, raising odds BTC tops $109,999 in 2026.
- 2026-05-01 [A] 05.01.2026 - Amendment to trading hours to allow for product specific trading hours - FEX — CFTC lets FEX set product-specific trading hours; could modestly affect Bitcoin trading windows/liquidity and slightly raise chance CF index exceeds $109,999, but not determinative.
- 2026-05-01 [background] 05.01.2026 - Issuance of CME Group Market Regulation Advisory Notice RA2602-5 Regarding CME, CBOT, NYMEX/COMEX Rule 575. ("Disruptive Practices Prohibited"). - COMEX — CFTC advisory on CME disruptive-practices may modestly affect derivatives liquidity/volatility, indirectly shifting odds BTC exceeds $109,999; not determinative.
- 2026-04-21 [A] Attorney General James Sues Coinbase and Gemini for Running Illegal Gambling Platforms in New York — AG lawsuit vs. Coinbase/Gemini raises regulatory/liquidity risk and could trigger sell‑offs, lowering the chance BTC breaches $109,999 in 2026.
- 2026-04-16 [background] CME | Modifications to the Event Contract… - 04/16/2026 — CFTC notice of CME contract modifications may modestly affect Bitcoin derivatives liquidity/price discovery and so slightly alter probability CF index breaches $110k.
- 2026-04-16 [A] COIN | Modifications to implement hard limits… - 04/16/2026 — CFTC modifications to Coinbase derivatives hard limits may affect BTC liquidity/volatility on Coinbase, slightly changing probability of briefly exceeding $109,999.
- 2026-03-23 [A] 91 FR 13714 - 3/23/2026 - Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets (PDF) — SEC Federal Register notice clarifying securities-law treatment of crypto assets could materially alter institutional demand and Bitcoin price, shifting odds of >$109,999.
- 2026-03-19 [A] Regulatory Capital: Category I and II Banking Organizations, Banking Organizations With Significant Trading Activity, and Optional Adoption for Other Banking Organizations — OCC capital rules for trading banks could change banks' crypto trading capacity and liquidity, modestly affecting Bitcoin price but indirectly.
- 2026-03-19 [A] Regulatory Capital Rule: Category I and II Banking Organizations, Banking Organizations with Significant Trading Activity, and Optional Adoption for Other Banking Organizations — FDIC capital rules for banks with significant trading activity could alter banks' crypto market‑making/liquidity, modestly affecting BTC's chance to top $109,999.
- 2026-02-12 [A] CFE Cboe Bitcoin Continuous Futures Lead Market Maker Program Amendment [2] — CFE lead market-maker amendment affects Bitcoin futures liquidity and could indirectly influence spot CF RT Index price, but doesn't directly determine a >$109,999.99 print.
- 2026-02-03 [background] ICE US New Cryptocurrency Futures Market Maker Program — CFTC/ICE market-maker program could boost crypto futures liquidity and price discovery on ICE, modestly raising the chance BTC breaches $109,999.99.
- 2026-02-03 [A] ICE US New Cryptocurrency Futures Liquidity Provider Program — CFTC/ICE liquidity-provider program for crypto futures may improve futures liquidity and price discovery, modestly increasing chance BTC exceeds $109,999 in the window.
- 2026-01-08 [A] COIN Modifications to the Non-Crypto Market Maker Program — CFTC modifications to Coinbase's market-maker program could alter liquidity/volatility on Coinbase, indirectly affecting the CF Bitcoin spot price vs $109,999.99.
- 2025-12-17 [A] CME - Modifications to the Cryptocurrency Options Market Maker Program — CME changes to crypto options market-maker program could modestly affect liquidity, hedging flows and BTC spot volatility — small impact on chances of >$109,999.
- 2025-12-17 [background] CME - Modifications to the Bitcoin Friday Futures Market Maker Program — Modifying CME's bitcoin futures market-maker program may change futures liquidity/volatility and thus modestly affect the odds BTC surpasses $110k in 2026.
- 2025-12-08 [A] Acting Chairman Pham Announces Launch of Digital Assets Pilot Program for Tokenized Collateral in Derivatives Markets — CFTC pilot enabling tokenized collateral for derivatives could boost institutional demand and liquidity for BTC, modestly raising odds it tops $109,999 in 2026.

response.text: Regulatory signals are mixed: CME/Cboe/ICE programs and extended trading hours bolster derivatives liquidity, while a Nasdaq filing for a BlackRock iShares Bitcoin Premium Income ETF could add spot demand. Offsetting headwinds include the NY AG's suit against Coinbase/Gemini and SEC guidance on crypto assets, plus bank capital rules that may constrain activity. Key outcomes - SEC action on the ETF, litigation results, and banks' implementation - remain pending.
